also: Endpoint Detection & Response, Endpoint Detection and Response
electronic-health-reporter other
electronic-health-reporter appears to be a named account, project, tool, or identifier referenced in security reporting.
EMEA concept
EMEA means Europe, the Middle East, and Africa, a common business region label.
EMEA Lead other
EMEA Lead is a job role responsible for activities in Europe, the Middle East, and Africa.
Emerging Threat Advisory concept
An Emerging Threat Advisory is a warning or report about a new or developing cybersecurity risk.
employees concept
Employees are people who work for an organization.
environment variables concept
Environment variables are named settings stored by an operating system or runtime that programs can read while they run.
Errol Weiss other
Errol Weiss is a named person quoted in the article.
escape_quotes() tool
escape_quotes() is a function name that suggests it changes quotation marks so text is safer to process in code.
eSentire organization
eSentire is a cybersecurity company that provides managed detection and response services.
eSentire TRU organization
eSentire TRU is eSentire's Threat Response Unit, a security research and incident response team.
eSentire's Threat Response Unit organization
eSentire's Threat Response Unit is a security team that investigates threats and responds to cyber incidents.
also: TRU, Canadian cybersecurity company
eset
ESET is a cybersecurity company that makes antivirus, endpoint protection, and threat research products.
eval() technique
eval() is a programming function that runs text as code, which can be dangerous if attackers control the text.
Everest threat-actor
Everest is a ransomware or extortion group name referenced in security reporting.
EvilTokens tool
EvilTokens is a named tool or technique referenced in security reporting for abusing or stealing authentication tokens.
also: EvilTokens platform
Excessively high privileges concept
Excessively high privileges means an account or program has more permissions than it needs, increasing the damage if it is abused.
also: excessive privileges
Execution policy bypasses technique
Execution policy bypasses are ways to get around settings that are supposed to restrict script execution, especially in PowerShell.
Exploits in the wild concept
Exploits in the wild means real attackers are already using a vulnerability outside a lab or proof-of-concept setting.
also: exploits in the wild
export controls concept
Export controls are government rules that restrict sending certain technologies, goods, or information to other countries or people.
also: controls, emergency controls
external control of the file name of the path vulnerability
External control of the file name or path is a vulnerability where an attacker can influence which file a program opens, writes, or deletes.
fable-5 product
fable-5 appears to refer to a specific AI model or release name, but I cannot define it confidently.
Fair Credit Reporting Act concept
The Fair Credit Reporting Act is a U.S. law that regulates credit reporting and gives consumers rights over their credit information.
also: FCRA
Fake .woff2 font files technique
Fake .woff2 font files are files disguised as web font files but used to hide or deliver malicious content.
also: fake-font payloads, fake `.woff2` font file
Famous Chollima threat-actor
Famous Chollima is a North Korea-linked threat actor or activity cluster referenced in security reporting.
fbi organization
The FBI is the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation, which investigates cybercrime and national security threats.
FDA organization
The FDA, or U.S. Food and Drug Administration, is the U.S. agency that regulates food, drugs, and many medical devices.
Federal Trade Commission organization
The Federal Trade Commission is a United States government agency that handles consumer protection issues.
fifa-world-cup
The FIFA World Cup is a major international soccer tournament organized by FIFA.
File hashes concept
File hashes are unique-looking digital fingerprints used to identify files and check whether they have changed.
File system concept
A file system is the way an operating system organizes and stores files on a disk or device.
also: operating system’s file system
File System Access API protocol
The File System Access API is a browser feature that lets websites read or write local files with user permission.
also: picker-based File System Access API
FileFix technique
FileFix is a social-engineering attack technique that tricks users into pasting or running a malicious file path or command.
fileless-execution
Fileless execution is running malicious code mainly in memory or through legitimate tools instead of saving obvious malware files to disk.
Financial Conduct Authority organization
The Financial Conduct Authority is the United Kingdom regulator that oversees financial firms and markets.
Firefox product
A free and open-source web browser developed by Mozilla, often targeted by attackers through malicious extensions or phishing.
Five Eyes organization
Five Eyes is an intelligence-sharing alliance between the United States, United Kingdom, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand.
Fj5NmEsp9EuKrun.ps1 tool
Fj5NmEsp9EuKrun.ps1 is a PowerShell script file name referenced in security reporting.
Force pushes technique
Force pushes are Git operations that overwrite remote commit history, which can remove or replace previous changes.
force-push technique
A Git command that overwrites remote repository history, sometimes used by attackers to remove traces or disrupt code after gaining access.
foreign national concept
A foreign national is a person who is not a citizen or national of the country being discussed.
FortiBleed other
FortiBleed is the name used in the article for a data leak involving Fortinet device credentials.
also: FortiBleed credential theft campaign, FortiBleed campaign, campaign
fortigate product
FortiGate is Fortinet’s line of firewall and network security appliances.
also: FortiGate firewalls, compromised FortiGate firewalls
FortiGate configuration files product
FortiGate configuration files store the settings for Fortinet FortiGate firewall devices.
also: downloaded FortiGate configuration files
FortiGate Sniffer tool
FortiGate Sniffer is a packet-capture feature or tool used on FortiGate devices to inspect network traffic.
fortinet organization
Fortinet is a cybersecurity company known for firewalls, VPNs, and network security products.
also: Fortinet devices, Fortinet credentials
FortiWeb product
FortiWeb is Fortinet's web application firewall product for protecting websites and web applications.
Francesco Bailo other
Francesco Bailo is a named person referenced in security or technology reporting.
also: University of Sydney AI governance researcher Francesco Bailo
fraud concept
Fraud is dishonest activity intended to trick someone for money, access, or other benefit.
Fraud detection engines tool
Fraud detection engines are systems that analyze activity to spot transactions or accounts that may be fraudulent.
Fraud Tools, Tactics, and Techniques concept
Fraud Tools, Tactics, and Techniques is a phrase for the methods and resources criminals use to commit fraud.
also: FT3, Stripe’s FT3 framework
fraudulent transactions concept
Fraudulent transactions are unauthorized or deceptive payments, transfers, or trades made to steal money or assets.
frint tool
frint is a named software package or tool referenced in security reporting.
FTC guidance concept
FTC guidance is advice or direction from the U.S. Federal Trade Commission about following consumer protection and privacy rules.
fulcrumsec other
fulcrumsec is a named security-related account, group, or tool referenced in security reporting.
Full cluster takeover technique
Full cluster takeover means an attacker gains control over an entire group of connected servers, such as a Kubernetes cluster.
generate-manifest tool
generate-manifest is a tool or command name that suggests creating a manifest file listing software resources or configuration.
GenerateManifest tool
GenerateManifest is a named software component or function referenced in security reporting.
also: GenerateManifest service
Generative Engine Optimization concept
Generative Engine Optimization is the practice of shaping content so generative AI systems are more likely to mention, summarize, or cite it.
also: GEO
geofencing technique
Geofencing is restricting or changing access based on a user's physical location or apparent internet location.
germany
Germany is a European country often referenced in cybersecurity reporting as a target, regulator, or law-enforcement partner.
GIPHY product
GIPHY is an online service for finding and sharing animated GIF images.
Git history rewriting technique
Git history rewriting changes past commits in a Git repository, which can hide or alter what previously happened in the project.
also: rewritten history, repository history has been rewritten
Git repositories concept
Git repositories are storage locations that keep source code and track changes to it over time.
also: Git repository
GitHub product
GitHub is a platform for storing, managing, and collaborating on software code.
also: GitHub repositories
GitHub Actions product
GitHub Actions is GitHub's automation service for building, testing, and deploying code when repository events happen.
github-enterprise product
github-enterprise is a software package or repository name related to GitHub Enterprise features or management.
Go modules tool
Go modules are the dependency and version management system used by the Go programming language.
also: Go module
Google organization
Google is a technology company that provides online services, including email-related services.
also: Google’s trusted infrastructure
Google Chrome product
Google Chrome is a widely used web browser made by Google.
Google Voice product
Google Voice is a Google service that provides phone numbers, calling, voicemail, and text messaging.
Google Voice numbers product
Google Voice numbers are phone numbers provided through Google's Voice calling and messaging service.
GPT-4o-mini product
GPT-4o-mini is an OpenAI language model designed for lower-cost AI tasks.
GPT-5.5 product
An unreleased or speculative intermediate version of OpenAI's Generative Pre-trained Transformer model, expected to bridge capabilities between GPT-5 and GPT-6.
GPT-5.6 product
GPT-5.6 is a named AI model version referenced in security reporting.
Grandoreiro malware
Grandoreiro is a Latin American banking-trojan malware family used to steal financial information and online banking access.
grant type protocol
A grant type is an OAuth method that defines how an application gets an access token.
Groq-hosted Llama models product
Groq-hosted Llama models are Meta Llama AI models served through Groq's cloud inference platform.
also: Llama models
grpc
gRPC is a framework that lets software services communicate efficiently with each other over a network.
also: internal gRPC service
GSSAPI protocol
GSSAPI is a standard interface that software can use to add authentication and secure communication features.
Guildma malware
Guildma is a Latin American banking-trojan malware family used to steal financial credentials and related information.
HackerOne organization
HackerOne is a platform that helps organizations run bug bounty and vulnerability disclosure programs.
HackerOne program product
A HackerOne program is a bug bounty or vulnerability disclosure program hosted on the HackerOne platform.
hackers concept
Individuals who use technical skills to gain unauthorized access to computer systems, or security researchers who test defenses.
also: threat actor
hard drives product
Hard drives are storage devices that keep files and programs on a computer.
also: two 2-terabyte hard drives, drives
Hard-coded Discord webhook technique
A hard-coded Discord webhook is a fixed Discord upload URL embedded in code so data can be sent to a Discord channel.
also: Discord webhook
Harden-Runner tool
Harden-Runner is a security tool for monitoring and restricting GitHub Actions workflow behavior.
harrods organization
Harrods is a luxury department store in London and a well-known retail organization.
health-isac
Health-ISAC is an information-sharing group that helps healthcare organizations exchange cyber threat intelligence.
healthcare-related statements concept
Healthcare-related statements are records or communications that describe medical, insurance, or care-related information.
Heap memory concept
Heap memory is a part of a program's memory used for data that is created and managed while the program runs.
also: adjacent heap memory
helios-aidr tool
helios-aidr is a security tool or project name referenced in reporting.
also: AIDR, AI Detection and Response
Helm tool
Helm is a tool used to package, install, and manage applications on Kubernetes.
also: helm binary
Helm chart tool
A Helm chart is a package of files that tells Helm how to install and configure an application on Kubernetes.
also: chart
help desk organization
A help desk is a support team that assists users with technical or account problems.
also: company's IT help desk
help-desk impersonation technique
Help-desk impersonation is when attackers pretend to be support staff to trick people into giving access or information.
Helsinki Airport organization
Helsinki Airport is the main international airport serving Helsinki, Finland.
HEUR:Trojan.ANSI.MalClaw.gen malware
HEUR:Trojan.ANSI.MalClaw.gen is a heuristic antivirus detection name for a suspected Trojan-style malicious file.
high-privilege accounts concept
High-privilege accounts are user accounts with powerful permissions that can change systems, access sensitive data, or manage other users.
High-severity vulnerabilities vulnerability
High-severity vulnerabilities are serious software weaknesses that could cause major security harm if exploited.
Hijack the agent technique
Hijack the agent means taking control of an automated software agent so it performs actions chosen by an attacker.
Historical data breaches concept
Historical data breaches are past incidents where private information was exposed or stolen.
Homeland Security Information Network organization
The Homeland Security Information Network is a U.S. Department of Homeland Security information-sharing platform for trusted partners.
Howard Lutnick other
Howard Lutnick is a U.S. business executive and public figure referenced in business or government reporting.
also: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick
HSIN-Intel organization
HSIN-Intel is an intelligence-sharing area or capability associated with the U.S. Homeland Security Information Network.
HTTP Profile concept
An HTTP Profile is a configuration object that controls how a device or service handles HTTP web traffic.
HTTP server tool
An HTTP server is software that sends web pages or web data to browsers and applications over the web.
HTTP Strict Profiles concept
HTTP Strict Profiles are hardened web-traffic settings that enforce stricter rules for handling HTTP connections.
HTTP/2 protocol
HTTP/2 is a newer version of the web protocol that helps browsers and servers exchange web content more efficiently.
Http2SmallWndTimeout concept
Http2SmallWndTimeout is a setting or event name related to HTTP/2 connection behavior when a small flow-control window causes a timeout.
Human traffic concept
Human traffic is website activity generated by real people rather than automated bots.
also: humans
Human viruses concept
Human viruses are biological viruses that infect people, not computer systems.
Humans of Talos other
Humans of Talos is a Cisco Talos content series highlighting people involved in security research.
huntress organization
Huntress is a cybersecurity company providing managed detection and response for small and midsize organizations.
Hybrid deployments concept
Hybrid deployments are setups where software runs across both cloud services and an organization's own systems.
IBM organization
IBM is a major technology company that provides hardware, software, cloud, and security services.
IBM 2025 organization
IBM 2025 appears to refer to a 2025 report, product release, or security research item from IBM.
Icarus threat-actor
Icarus is the extortion group named in the article as claiming responsibility for the attack.
identities concept
Identities are accounts or actors that systems recognize and allow to access resources.
also: identity
Identity fraud concept
Identity fraud is when someone uses another person's personal information to pretend to be them, usually to steal money or get services.
identity providers product
Identity providers are systems that manage sign-ins and confirm who a user is.
identity theft concept
Identity theft is when someone uses another person’s personal information without permission, often to commit fraud.
improper input validation vulnerability
Improper input validation is a software flaw where a program fails to properly check data it receives, which can let attackers cause harmful behavior.
In-Browser Ransomware technique
In-browser ransomware is ransomware-like activity that runs inside a web browser instead of as a traditional installed program.
also: browser-native ransomware
INC threat-actor
INC is a ransomware or data-extortion group name referenced in security reporting.
also: INC ransomware-as-a-service, INC ransomware, INC ransomware gang
INC Ransom threat-actor
INC Ransom is a ransomware and data-extortion group referenced in security reporting.
INC ransomware leak site tool
The INC ransomware leak site is a website used by the INC extortion group to publish or threaten to publish stolen victim data.
incorrect authorization vulnerability
Incorrect authorization is a vulnerability where a system wrongly allows someone to access or do something they should not be permitted to do.
Indicator-based blocking technique
Indicator-based blocking stops known-bad items such as IP addresses, domains, URLs, or file hashes.
indicator-of-attack concept
An indicator-of-attack is a sign of attacker behavior, such as suspicious commands or movement, that may reveal an attack in progress.
also: IOA, indicator-of-attack (IOA)
InfernoGrabber malware
InfernoGrabber is a malware family or credential-stealing tool referenced in security reporting.
also: InfernoGrabber v9.0
information stealer malware
An information stealer is malware designed to collect and steal data from a device.
infostealer malware
An infostealer is malware designed to secretly collect passwords, browser data, and other valuable information.
also: stealer
infostealer compromise concept
An infostealer compromise is an incident where malware steals saved passwords, cookies, or other sensitive data from a device.
Inject commands technique
Inject commands means sneak attacker-controlled instructions into input so a system runs them as commands.
install.res.1033.dll malware
install.res.1033.dll is a Windows DLL-style file name referenced as a security artifact.
also: rogue install.res.1033.dll library
installer_method3_stream.vbs tool
installer_method3_stream.vbs is a Visual Basic Script file name referenced in security reporting, likely used as an installer script.
InstallUtil tool
InstallUtil is a Microsoft .NET utility for installing assemblies that attackers can abuse to execute code.
installutil.exe tool
installutil.exe is a Microsoft .NET utility for installing assemblies that attackers can abuse to execute code.
insufficient input validation vulnerability
A common security flaw where user-supplied data is not properly checked, leading to injections, buffer overflows, or other exploits.
also: insufficient input validation vulnerability
internal network port concept
An internal network port is a numbered communication endpoint on a private network service that may not be exposed to the internet.
Internet concept
The internet is the global network that connects computers, websites, services, and people around the world.
internet infrastructure provider organization
An internet infrastructure provider is a company that supplies core services such as hosting, networking, DNS, or traffic routing.
INTERPOL organization
INTERPOL is an international police organization that helps countries cooperate on cross-border crime, including cybercrime.
Interpol Red Notice concept
An Interpol Red Notice is an international request to law enforcement to locate and provisionally arrest a wanted person.
also: international arrest request
iOS product
iOS is Apple's operating system for iPhones.
IP context analysis technique
IP context analysis means looking at details about an internet address, such as location, network, and history, to judge risk.
IPv6 address range concept
An IPv6 address range is a block of internet addresses using the newer IPv6 addressing system.
also: IPv6 range
iran
Iran is a country often referenced in cybersecurity reporting involving espionage, cyber operations, and regional targeting.
ironscales organization
IRONSCALES is an email security company focused on phishing detection and response.
IT administrators concept
IT administrators are people responsible for managing an organization's computers, networks, and accounts.
IT help desk organization
An IT help desk is the support team that helps users with computer, account, and technology problems.
Ivanti Sentry product
Ivanti Sentry is a security gateway product used to help protect and manage access to mobile and enterprise services.
jailbreak
A jailbreak is a method of bypassing restrictions on a device or AI system to gain capabilities that are normally blocked.
also: prompt, prompt trick, safety bypass
japan
Japan is an East Asian country often referenced in cybersecurity stories involving technology, companies, or government activity.
Java web application concept
A Java web application is a website or online service built using the Java programming language.
Javali malware
Javali is a Latin American banking-trojan malware family used to target financial users and steal banking-related information.
JavaScript concept
JavaScript is a programming language commonly used to build software and web applications.
also: JavaScript file
jfrog
JFrog is a company that provides software supply-chain and package management tools such as Artifactory.
JNDIRealm product
JNDIRealm is an Apache Tomcat component that checks user identities against a directory service using Java's JNDI system.
also: Realm, affected Realm
joint U.S. and international advisory concept
A joint U.S. and international advisory is a public warning or guidance document issued together by U.S. and foreign authorities.
also: advisory
Joomla SP Page Builder product
Joomla SP Page Builder is an extension used to build website pages in the Joomla content management system.
JPMorgan Chase organization
JPMorgan Chase is a major global financial services and banking company.
Justice Department organization
The Justice Department is the United States federal agency responsible for enforcing federal law.
JWT concept
A compact token format for securely transmitting claims between parties, widely used for authentication, but dangerous if misconfigured.
Kaspersky organization
Kaspersky is a cybersecurity company known for antivirus and other security products.
also: Russian cybersecurity company
Kaspersky products product
Kaspersky products are cybersecurity tools from Kaspersky, such as antivirus, endpoint protection, and scanning products.
Kaspersky Scan Engine product
Kaspersky Scan Engine is a Kaspersky product that lets other systems scan files and objects for malware.
also: Scan Engine
KB5095093 product
A Microsoft knowledge base article describing a specific Windows update, often related to security fixes.
KDDI organization
KDDI is a major Japanese telecommunications company that provides mobile, internet, and related communications services.
Kemp LoadMaster product
Kemp LoadMaster is a load-balancing and application delivery product now associated with Progress.
Kerberos protocol
Kerberos is a network authentication protocol that lets users and services prove their identity securely using tickets.
also: Kerberos-backed LDAP
Keys concept
Keys are secret or identifying values used to unlock accounts, decrypt data, or prove access.
Keystrokes concept
Keystrokes are the keys someone presses on a keyboard, which malware may try to record to steal passwords or messages.
Kimi K2.7 product
Kimi K2.7 is a named AI model or product referenced in security reporting.
Kimsuky threat-actor
Kimsuky is a North Korea-linked cyber-espionage threat group known for targeting governments, researchers, and other organizations.
Kohl's organization
Kohl's is a U.S. retail chain that sells clothing, home goods, and other consumer products.
Kohl's organization
Kohl's is a U.S. retail company that may be mentioned as a brand, target, or lure in security reporting.
Kohl's Department Stores organization
Kohl's Department Stores is a U.S. retail chain that sells clothing, home goods, and other consumer products.
Kroll organization
Kroll is a risk and financial advisory company that provides services including cybersecurity incident response and breach support.
Kroll identity protection product
Kroll identity protection is a service that helps people monitor and respond to possible identity theft after data exposure.
Kubernetes product
Kubernetes is a platform for running and managing containerized applications across many servers.
also: Kubernetes clusters
Kubernetes manifests tool
Kubernetes manifests are configuration files that tell Kubernetes what applications and settings to run in a cluster.
also: manifests
Kubota Corporation organization
Kubota Corporation is a Japanese company that makes machinery such as tractors and industrial equipment.
Kubota North America Corporation organization
Kubota North America Corporation is the North American business arm of Kubota, a manufacturer of tractors and industrial equipment.
also: Kubota North America, Kubota
Kubota USA organization
Kubota USA is a U.S. business name associated with Kubota's equipment operations in the United States.
also: Kubota USA site
Kustomize tool
Kustomize is a Kubernetes configuration tool used to customize deployment files without editing the originals directly.
KYC concept
KYC, short for Know Your Customer, is the process organizations use to verify a customer's identity and reduce fraud or abuse.
Large Language Models concept
Large language models are AI systems trained on huge amounts of text so they can generate and understand human-like language.
also: LLMs
lateral movement technique
Lateral movement is moving from one connected system to another after gaining access somewhere.
law enforcement agencies organization
Law enforcement agencies are organizations responsible for enforcing laws and investigating crime.
also: law enforcement agents
LDAP protocol
Lightweight Directory Access Protocol, used for accessing and maintaining distributed directory information services like Active Directory.
Leaked credentials concept
Leaked credentials are usernames, passwords, or login tokens that have been exposed to people who should not have them.
Lighthouse tool
Lighthouse is a named tool or product referenced in security reporting.
Living Security organization
Living Security is a cybersecurity company focused on security awareness and human risk management.
living-off-the-land technique
Living-off-the-land is when attackers use built-in system tools instead of obvious malware to hide their activity.
LLM architectures concept
LLM architectures are the technical designs used to build large language models that generate and understand text.
Load balancer application tool
A load balancer application spreads incoming traffic across multiple servers so one server does not get overwhelmed.
localStorage tool
localStorage is a browser feature that lets websites save data on a user's device for later use.
Lodash product
Lodash is a popular JavaScript utility library used by developers to simplify common programming tasks.
logcrypt-cryptography tool
logcrypt-cryptography is a named software package or tool referenced in security reporting.
login concept
A login is the process of entering information, such as a username and password, to access an account.
also: logins
London's transport agency organization
London's transport agency is the organization responsible for running and managing public transport services in London.
Low-complexity attacks concept
Low-complexity attacks are attacks that are relatively easy to carry out because they require few special conditions or skills.
LSHIY LLC organization
LSHIY LLC is a named company referenced in security reporting.
also: LSHIY
Lucid tool
Lucid is a named tool, product, or organization referenced in security reporting.
lynx
Lynx is a text-based web browser used from a command line.
also: Lynx ransomware, Lynx ransomware-as-a-service, Lynx / INC ransomware group
macOS product
macOS is Apple’s operating system for Mac computers.
Malicious concept
Malicious means intentionally harmful, such as software or behavior designed to damage, steal, or deceive.
also: known-bad
Malicious Android app malware
A malicious Android app is an app for Android devices that is designed to steal data, spy on users, or perform other harmful actions.
also: Android app
Malicious skills technique
Malicious skills are add-ons or capabilities designed to make an AI agent or assistant perform harmful actions.
malware concept
Malware is software made to harm, spy on, or take control of computers or data.
Man-in-the-Middle technique
A Man-in-the-Middle attack happens when an attacker secretly intercepts or alters communication between two parties.
mandiant
Mandiant is a cybersecurity company known for incident response, threat intelligence, and breach investigations.
Mandiant 2026 organization
Mandiant 2026 appears to refer to a 2026 report, product release, or security research item from Mandiant.
Mapbox organization
Mapbox is a mapping and location-data platform used by developers to build maps into apps and websites.
Mapbox dataset product
A Mapbox dataset is a collection of location or map data stored and managed in the Mapbox platform.
also: Mapbox datasets
Marks & Spencer organization
A major British multinational retailer, often referenced in breach or security incident reporting.
martin-lee other
martin-lee appears to be a named account or identifier referenced in security reporting.
MasterPackager.Updater tool
MasterPackager.Updater is a software updater component or executable referenced in security reporting.
Mastra AI Framework tool
Mastra AI Framework is an AI software framework referenced in security reporting.
MCPoison tool
MCPoison is a named security tool, exploit, or attack technique referenced in reporting about Model Context Protocol environments.
Melcoz malware
Melcoz is a Latin American banking-trojan malware family used to steal financial credentials and related information.
memory overflow vulnerability
A memory overflow is a bug where a program writes or uses more memory than it should, which can crash software or create security risks.
also: memory overflow vulnerabilities
memory overread vulnerability
A memory overread is a bug where software reads more data from memory than it should, potentially exposing sensitive information.
also: out-of-bounds memory reads
MGM Resorts organization
MGM Resorts is a hospitality and entertainment company that operates hotels, casinos, and resorts.
also: MGM
Miasma tool
Miasma is a phishing kit or campaign name used in cybercrime reporting.
Microphone product
A microphone is a device that captures sound, which malware may try to access for spying.
microsoft organization
Microsoft is a major technology company that makes Windows, Office, Azure, and many enterprise security products.
also: tech giant
Microsoft accounts product
Microsoft accounts are user accounts used to sign in to Microsoft services such as Outlook, Windows, Xbox, and Azure-related tools.
also: accounts
Microsoft Azure product
Microsoft Azure is Microsoft's cloud computing platform.
Microsoft Conditional Access product
Microsoft Conditional Access is a Microsoft security feature that controls sign-ins based on conditions like user, device, location, and risk.
Microsoft Defender product
Microsoft Defender is Microsoft’s built-in security software for detecting and blocking malware and other threats.
Microsoft Entra ID product
Microsoft Entra ID is Microsoft's cloud identity service for managing users, logins, apps, and access permissions.
Microsoft install.exe binary tool
Microsoft install.exe binary refers to a Windows executable file named install.exe associated with Microsoft software or abused because it appears legitimate.
also: install.exe
Microsoft Quantum Safe Program organization
Microsoft Quantum Safe Program is Microsoft's effort to prepare its products and customers for cryptography that can withstand quantum-computer threats.
Microsoft Update Catalog product
Microsoft Update Catalog is Microsoft's website for manually downloading Windows and other Microsoft software updates.
microsoft-365
Microsoft 365 is Microsoft’s cloud productivity and security suite including Office apps, email, storage, and collaboration tools.
microsoft-azure-cli tool
microsoft-azure-cli is a software package or repository name for Microsoft's Azure command-line tooling.
microsoft-sharepoint
Microsoft SharePoint is Microsoft’s document collaboration and intranet platform.
microsoft.com/devicelogin product
microsoft.com/devicelogin is Microsoft's device login page used to sign in devices through a browser code flow.
Mirai malware
Mirai is a malware family that infects internet-connected devices and uses them in botnets for attacks such as DDoS.
missing release of memory after effective lifetime vulnerability
Missing release of memory after effective lifetime is a memory-management flaw where software fails to free memory it no longer needs.
mitiga
Mitiga is a cloud incident response and threat detection company.
MITRE ATT&CK tool
MITRE ATT&CK is a framework for naming and organizing attacker techniques.
model-context-protocol
Model Context Protocol is a standard that lets AI applications connect to external tools, data, and services.
Model-generated links concept
Model-generated links are web links created or suggested by an AI model, which may be wrong, unsafe, or invented.
moltbot malware
A bot or malware family referenced in security reporting, likely used for automated attacks or data exfiltration.
MongoBleed vulnerability
MongoBleed is a named vulnerability or attack reference associated with MongoDB-related data exposure.
Montana Empire tool
Montana Empire is a named group, campaign, or entity referenced in security reporting.
mora1987.work.gd other
mora1987.work.gd is a domain indicator referenced in security reporting.
MSBuild tool
MSBuild is Microsoft’s build tool for compiling software projects, and attackers sometimes abuse it to run malicious code.
msbuild.exe tool
msbuild.exe is a Microsoft build tool for compiling projects that attackers can abuse to run malicious code.
msgbox.txt other
msgbox.txt is a text file name referenced in security reporting, possibly used to store messages or instructions.
Muddywater threat-actor
A suspected Iranian state-sponsored cyber espionage group known for targeting Middle Eastern governments and telecoms.
multi-factor authentication concept
Multi-factor authentication is a login method that asks users to prove who they are in more than one way.
also: MFA
multifactor authentication technique
Multifactor authentication requires more than one proof of identity to log in, such as a password plus another verification method.
Mythos 5 product
Mythos 5 is a named AI model or model version referenced in reporting.
also: Mythos
National Association of Insurance Commissioners organization
The National Association of Insurance Commissioners is a U.S. organization of state insurance regulators that helps coordinate insurance rules and standards.
Natural language concept
Natural language is normal human language, such as English or Spanish, rather than programming code.
also: natural language instructions
NetScaler ADC product
NetScaler ADC is an application delivery controller product used to manage, optimize, and secure traffic to web applications.
also: Citrix ADC
NetScaler Gateway product
NetScaler Gateway is a remote access and application access product used to let users securely connect to internal applications.
also: Citrix Gateway
network intrusions concept
Network intrusions are unauthorized entries into computer networks.
Network Operations Management product
Network Operations Management is software or a process for monitoring and managing computer networks and related services.
Network policies concept
Network policies are rules that control which systems or services are allowed to communicate with each other.
also: Kubernetes network policies, policy files
network systems concept
Network systems are the devices, software, and services that connect computers and allow them to communicate.
network traffic concept
Network traffic is the data moving between computers, servers, phones, and other devices over a network.
networkPolicy.create concept
networkPolicy.create is a configuration setting that controls whether a Kubernetes network policy should be created.
Nextcloud product
Nextcloud is a self-hosted cloud platform for file storage, sharing, collaboration, and related services.
Nextcloud zero-day vulnerability
Nextcloud zero-day refers to a previously unknown vulnerability affecting Nextcloud that attackers may exploit before a fix is available.
ngrok tool
ngrok is a tool that creates secure tunnels from the public internet to services running on a local or private machine.
Nidec organization
Nidec is a Japanese manufacturer of electric motors and related components.
Nissan organization
Nissan is a global automobile manufacturer that may appear in security or data-breach stories.
Node.js product
Node.js is a platform that lets developers run JavaScript code outside a web browser, often on servers.
also: node
Non-AI bots tool
Non-AI bots are automated programs that follow fixed rules rather than using artificial intelligence to decide what to do.
non-human identities concept
Non-human identities are digital identities used by systems, services, or automated processes instead of people.
also: machine identities
North Korean threat actors threat-actor
North Korean threat actors are hacking groups linked by researchers or governments to North Korea.
also: North Korea-linked, North Korean
north-korea other
North Korea is a country often referenced in cybersecurity reports because some hacking groups are alleged to operate on its behalf.
Northern District of Illinois organization
The Northern District of Illinois is a U.S. federal judicial district that handles federal legal cases in part of Illinois.
Nottingham University organization
Nottingham University is a university referenced in reporting or research.
Novant Health organization
Novant Health is an organization named in the article.
npm product
npm is the main package manager and public registry for JavaScript software libraries.
npm packages product
npm packages are reusable JavaScript software modules distributed through the npm package ecosystem.
Null-terminate sanitized strings technique
Null-terminate sanitized strings means ending cleaned-up text with a special zero byte so software knows where the string stops.
NVD organization
The National Vulnerability Database, or NVD, is a U.S. government database that tracks publicly known software vulnerabilities.
NVIDIA organization
NVIDIA is a technology company known for graphics chips, AI hardware, and related software.
NVIDIA SkillSpector tool
NVIDIA SkillSpector is a named NVIDIA research tool or system referenced in reporting about inspecting AI agent skills.
OAuth protocol
An authorization protocol that allows a user to grant a third-party application limited access to their resources without sharing their password.
also: OAuth 2.1
OAuth 2.0 protocol
OAuth 2.0 is a standard that lets apps get limited access to an account without sharing the user's password with the app.
OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant protocol
OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant is a login flow that lets devices with limited input, like TVs, sign in through another device such as a phone or computer.
also: Device Authorization Grant
OAuth apps protocol
OAuth apps are applications that use OAuth to request limited access to a user's account or data without directly sharing the user's password.
oauth-token-theft
OAuth token theft is when attackers steal access tokens that let them use online accounts without knowing the password.
Obfuscated JavaScript loaders malware
Obfuscated JavaScript loaders are hidden or hard-to-read scripts that fetch or start additional malicious code in a browser.
also: obfuscated JavaScript loader, JavaScript malware loader
OBS Studio product
OBS Studio is free open-source software used for screen recording and live streaming.
Octo Tempest threat-actor
Octo Tempest is a financially motivated threat actor known for social engineering, identity attacks, and extortion activity.
Office of Intelligence and Analysis organization
The Office of Intelligence and Analysis is a U.S. Department of Homeland Security office that produces and shares intelligence information.
OmniStealer malware
OmniStealer is an information-stealing malware family referenced in security reporting.
On-premises Adobe Campaign instances product
On-premises Adobe Campaign instances are installations of Adobe Campaign that run on a customer's own servers instead of Adobe's cloud.
also: on-premises Adobe Campaign, on-premises instances, on-premises deployments
One-time passcodes concept
One-time passcodes are temporary codes used once to help verify a login or transaction.
OneDrive product
OneDrive is Microsoft's cloud storage service for saving and syncing files online.
Online portals concept
Online portals are websites or apps where users log in to access services, accounts, or information.
openai organization
OpenAI is an AI company known for ChatGPT and GPT language models.
OpenClaw product
OpenClaw is a named tool or project, but I cannot define it confidently from the term alone.
openclaw-skills tool
openclaw-skills is a named package, repository, or toolset referenced in security reporting.
OpenCTI tool
OpenCTI is an open-source platform for organizing, analyzing, and sharing cyber threat intelligence.
OpenSSF Scorecard tool
OpenSSF Scorecard is a tool that checks open-source projects for security best practices.
Operating system command injection flaw vulnerability
An operating system command injection flaw lets an attacker make an application run unwanted system commands.
also: OS command injection flaw, OS Command Injection Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
operational or business disruptions concept
Operational or business disruptions are interruptions that stop an organization from working normally.
operational servers concept
Operational servers are live servers used to run real business services or applications.
also: servers, infrastructure
Opus 4.8 product
Opus 4.8 is a named AI model or model version in the higher-end Opus family of Claude-style systems.
oracle
Oracle is a technology company known for databases, enterprise software, cloud services, and Java-related products.
Oracle Payments product
Oracle Payments is an Oracle business software component used to manage payment processing within Oracle applications.
Oracle PeopleSoft Suite product
Oracle PeopleSoft Suite is enterprise business software used by organizations for tasks like HR, finance, and operations.
oracle-e-business-suite
Oracle E-Business Suite is Oracle’s enterprise software suite for finance, supply chain, human resources, and other business processes.
OrangeCon organization
OrangeCon is a cybersecurity conference where researchers and practitioners share security work.
oratomic other
oratomic is a named identifier, account, package, or artifact referenced in security reporting.
Organic traffic concept
Organic traffic is website traffic that arrives naturally from search results or links rather than paid ads.
OS command injection vulnerability
OS command injection is a flaw that lets an attacker make an application run unwanted operating system commands.
Otter.ai product
Otter.ai is a transcription and meeting-notes service that uses AI to turn speech into text.
Ousaban malware
Ousaban is a Latin American banking-trojan malware family associated with stealing online banking and financial information.
Out-of-band response process concept
An out-of-band response process is an emergency way to handle an issue outside normal procedures, often because speed is critical.
Out-of-bounds read vulnerability
An out-of-bounds read is a bug where software reads memory outside the area it is supposed to access.
Outdated concept
Outdated means software, data, or settings are old and may be missing important fixes or protections.
OWASP organization
OWASP is a nonprofit security organization best known for publishing widely used guidance on web application risks.
OWASP Dependency-Check tool
OWASP Dependency-Check is a tool that scans software dependencies for known vulnerable components.
OWASP Top 10 concept
OWASP Top 10 is a widely used list of the most important web application security risks.
P-256 protocol
P-256 is a widely used elliptic curve for public-key cryptography.
Packagist product
Packagist is the main public package repository used by Composer for PHP software libraries.
Palo Alto Networks organization
A leading global cybersecurity company known for next-generation firewalls and advanced cloud security.
Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 organization
Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 is a threat intelligence and incident response team within Palo Alto Networks.
PAN-OS product
PAN-OS is the operating system that runs Palo Alto Networks firewalls and security appliances.
Passkeys concept
Passkeys are a newer way to sign in using your device or biometrics instead of typing a password.
password concept
A password is a secret word or phrase used to prove you are allowed to access an account or system.
also: password resets, passwords
password hashes concept
Password hashes are scrambled versions of passwords that systems store so they do not have to keep the plain passwords.
Password manager tool
A password manager is an app that stores and helps create strong passwords so users do not have to remember them all.
password spray attack technique
A password spray attack tries one or a few common passwords against many accounts to avoid locking out a single account.
also: password spraying activity, credential spray attacks, credential spray attack, automated password spray attack
password-cracking technique
Password-cracking is the process of guessing or computing passwords, often using stolen password hashes or automated tools.
also: crack password hashes
password-spraying technique
Password spraying is when attackers try a few common passwords across many accounts to avoid quick lockouts.
path-traversal vulnerability
Path traversal is a vulnerability where attackers use file path tricks to access files they should not be able to reach.
Payment details concept
Payment details are information used to pay for something, such as card numbers, billing addresses, or bank account data.
Per-article concept
Per-article means something is measured or applied separately for each individual article.
persistent backdoor accounts technique
Persistent backdoor accounts are hidden or unauthorized accounts attackers create so they can keep getting back into a system later.
also: backdoor accounts
persistent unauthorized access technique
Persistent unauthorized access means an attacker can keep accessing a system without permission over time.
personal information concept
Personal information is data that can identify or describe a person, such as a name, address, phone number, or account details.
personalized notifications concept
Personalized notifications are messages tailored to a specific person's account, activity, or information.
also: notifications
Peter Stokes other
Peter Stokes is a named person referenced in reporting or legal documents.
also: Stokes, Bouquet, Spencer, Jordan
Phantom squatting technique
Phantom squatting is an attack idea where someone registers or creates resources that an AI or system falsely invents or recommends.
PhantomRaven threat-actor
PhantomRaven is a named campaign, threat actor, or malware-related label referenced in security reporting.
PhantomRaven campaign threat-actor
The PhantomRaven campaign is a named security campaign referenced in reporting, likely involving coordinated malicious activity.
phishing technique
Phishing is a scam where attackers impersonate trusted people or services to steal information or make victims take unsafe actions.
also: phishing campaigns, phishing pages, brand-impersonation phishing
Phishing decoy technique
A phishing decoy is fake content designed to trick a person into trusting a scam or revealing information.
phishing kit tool
A pre-packaged set of tools and templates that allows even non-technical criminals to launch phishing campaigns easily.
phishing technique technique
A phishing technique is a method attackers use to trick people into revealing passwords, clicking malicious links, or approving access.
phishing-as-a-service concept
Phishing-as-a-service is a criminal service that sells ready-made tools and infrastructure for running phishing attacks.
also: PhaaS
Picus organization
A cybersecurity company that provides automated breach and attack simulation to help organizations test their defenses continuously.
Piscina tool
Piscina is a Node.js library for running tasks in worker threads.
plaintext concept
Plaintext is information stored or sent in a readable, unprotected form.
also: plaintext format, text file
Plaintext files concept
Plaintext files store readable text without encryption or special protection.
plaintext Kubernetes secrets concept
Plaintext Kubernetes secrets are sensitive Kubernetes values, such as passwords or tokens, stored or exposed in readable form.
also: Kubernetes secrets
Pokka organization
Pokka is a beverage brand and company name referenced in security reporting.
PolinRider threat-actor
PolinRider is a named threat actor, malware, or activity label referenced in security reporting.
Portal Browser product
Portal Browser is a named browser or browser-like product referenced in security reporting.
Post-compromise activity technique
Post-compromise activity is what attackers do after they have already broken into a system.
postinstall-script technique
A postinstall script is code that runs automatically after a software package is installed, which can be abused to execute malware.
powershell
PowerShell is Microsoft’s command-line shell and scripting language, often used by administrators and abused by attackers.
also: PowerShell script, PowerShell processes, PowerShell loader, powershell.exe
PowerShell runspace concept
A PowerShell runspace is an execution environment where PowerShell commands or scripts can run inside a process.
pre-authentication concept
Pre-authentication means something happens before a user has logged in or proven who they are.
Primary Refresh Token protocol
A Primary Refresh Token is a Microsoft identity token that helps keep a user signed in to cloud-connected Windows services.
also: PRT
Prime membership product
Prime membership is Amazon's paid subscription service that offers benefits such as shipping, streaming, and deals.
also: Prime program, Prime
Priority 1 concept
Priority 1 usually means the most urgent level of issue that needs immediate attention.
privacy concept
Privacy is the ability to control who can access your personal information or activities.
Process hollowing technique
Process hollowing is a malware technique where attackers start a legitimate program and replace its contents with malicious code.
Programming languages concept
Programming languages are formal languages people use to write software instructions for computers.
Progress organization
Progress is a software company that makes business and infrastructure products.
Progress Kemp LoadMaster product
Progress Kemp LoadMaster is an application delivery and load-balancing product from Progress.
also: Progress LoadMaster, LoadMaster, LoadMaster appliance
Project Glasswing tool
Project Glasswing is a named project referenced in security reporting, with its specific purpose depending on the article's context.
prompt injection technique
Prompt injection is a way of giving an AI system instructions that may make it behave in unintended ways.
Prompt jailbreak technique
A prompt jailbreak is a trick that tries to make an AI system ignore its safety rules or restrictions.
Proof-of-concept exploit tool
A proof-of-concept exploit is sample code or a demonstration showing that a vulnerability can really be abused.
also: PoC exploit
proof-of-concept exploits tool
Proof-of-concept exploits are example programs or instructions that show a security flaw can be used in practice.
also: PoC exploits, PoCs, PoC
Proofpoint organization
Proofpoint is a cybersecurity company known for email security, threat intelligence, and data protection products.
Proposed order concept
A proposed order is a draft legal or regulatory settlement that sets out rules or penalties before it becomes final.
Prototype Pollution vulnerability
Prototype pollution is a JavaScript security flaw where attackers change shared object behavior in a way that can affect an application.
proxy concept
A proxy is an intermediate server that forwards internet traffic between a user and a destination.
Proxy injection technique
Proxy injection is a technique where an attacker inserts or manipulates proxy settings or traffic paths to intercept or redirect communications.
pull-request-target technique
pull-request-target is a GitHub Actions trigger that runs in the context of the target repository and can expose sensitive permissions if used carelessly.
PureLogs malware
PureLogs is an information-stealing malware family that collects sensitive data from infected systems.
PureLogs Stealer malware
PureLogs Stealer is an information-stealing malware family that collects sensitive data from infected systems.
pypi
PyPI is the main public package repository for Python software libraries.
Python tool
Python is a popular programming language often used to write scripts, automation tools, and security research code.
Python Flask application product
A Python Flask application is a web app built using the Flask framework for the Python programming language.
also: Python artifact
Python Package Index organization
The Python Package Index is the main public repository where Python developers publish and download software packages.
QR code concept
A QR code is a square barcode that people can scan with a phone to open a link or share information.
Quest Diagnostics organization
Quest Diagnostics is a large U.S. medical testing and diagnostics company.
ransom payments concept
Ransom payments are money paid to criminals in hopes of recovering data, stopping leaks, or restoring access after an attack.
ransomware concept
Ransomware is malicious software or an operation that locks or steals data to pressure victims into paying money.
also: ransomware operations, ransomware groups
ransomware decryption keys concept
Ransomware decryption keys are secret codes needed to unlock files encrypted by ransomware.
also: decryption keys
ransomware gangs threat-actor
Criminal groups that use malicious software to encrypt victims' data and demand payment for its release.
ransomware negotiation panels tool
Ransomware negotiation panels are online portals criminals use to chat with victims and demand payment.
also: negotiation panels, administration panels, negotiation dashboards
ransomware negotiations concept
Ransomware negotiations are discussions between victims and attackers over ransom demands, deadlines, and possible payment.
Ransomware toolkit tool
A ransomware toolkit is a set of tools used to create, deploy, or manage ransomware attacks.
ransomware-as-a-service concept
Ransomware-as-a-service is a criminal business model where ransomware developers rent their malware to other attackers.
also: RaaS, ransomware-as-a-service platform
React tool
A widely used open-source JavaScript library for building user interfaces, maintained by Meta.
also: React single-page application
React Router product
React Router is a JavaScript library used to manage page navigation in React web applications.
React2Shell vulnerability
React2Shell is a named vulnerability or exploit label associated with React-related security reporting.
Recurring subscriptions concept
Recurring subscriptions are services that automatically charge a customer at regular intervals until canceled.
Redis product
Redis is a fast in-memory database often used for caching, queues, and real-time application data.
also: Redis cache, Argo CD's Redis cache
Redis cache poisoning technique
Redis cache poisoning is an attack where false or malicious data is placed into a Redis cache so applications trust and use it.
Redis password concept
A Redis password is the secret used to restrict access to a Redis database server.
Reflective code loading technique
Reflective code loading is a technique that runs code directly from memory without loading it in the normal operating-system way.
also: reflective .NET loading
regasm tool
regasm is a Windows .NET registration utility that attackers can abuse to execute code through a trusted system tool.
regsvcs tool
regsvcs is a Windows .NET registration utility that attackers can abuse to run code through a trusted system tool.
regsvcs.exe tool
regsvcs.exe is a Windows .NET registration utility that attackers can abuse to run code through a trusted system tool.
remote access trojan malware
A remote access trojan is malware that secretly lets an attacker control a victim's computer from somewhere else.
also: RAT
remote-code-execution technique
Remote code execution is a serious flaw that lets an attacker run commands on a system from elsewhere.
also: RCE
repo-server tool
repo-server is a repository-service component name often used for software that fetches or processes application source configuration.
also: repo-server component
Report Fraud product
Report Fraud is a public service or portal name used for submitting fraud reports to authorities or organizations.
Reputation scores concept
Reputation scores are ratings used to judge whether something like an IP address, domain, account, or seller is likely trustworthy or risky.
Reputation-based defenses technique
Reputation-based defenses judge whether something is risky based on its known history, popularity, or trustworthiness.
Resource Owner Password Credentials protocol
Resource Owner Password Credentials is an OAuth grant type where an app directly receives a user's username and password to get an access token.
also: ROPC, Resource Owner Password Credentials (ROPC)
Retire.js tool
Retire.js is a security tool that finds outdated JavaScript libraries with known vulnerabilities.
Retrieval-Augmented Generation technique
A technique where an AI model first retrieves relevant facts from a database and then uses them when generating an answer, reducing mistakes.
Reverse proxies tool
Reverse proxies are servers that sit in front of web applications and forward requests to them, often adding security, caching, or load balancing.
RFC 8628 protocol
RFC 8628 is the official specification for the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant.
Ridge Security organization
Ridge Security is a cybersecurity company referenced in security reporting.
Rijndael concept
Rijndael is the encryption algorithm family from which AES was selected.
RSA-2048 protocol
RSA-2048 is a common public-key cryptography method using a 2048-bit key.
Rule engine tool
A rule engine is software that checks data against predefined rules and takes actions when matches are found.
RunMRU registry key concept
The RunMRU registry key is a Windows location that records commands recently typed into the Run dialog.
also: RunMRU
runtime detection and response concept
Runtime detection and response monitors software while it is running to spot and react to suspicious behavior in real time.
Runtime mutation technique
Runtime mutation is changing code or behavior while a program is running to evade detection or adapt actions.
also: polymorphism
russia
Russia is a country frequently referenced in cybersecurity stories involving cybercrime groups, espionage, and government activity.
SaaS concept
SaaS is software delivered over the internet as a subscription or hosted service instead of installed and managed entirely by the customer.
also: shadow SaaS
Safari product
Apple's web browser that comes pre-installed on Macs, iPhones, and iPads.
Safety rules concept
Safety rules are limits or instructions designed to stop a system, person, or AI model from causing harm.
also: safety guardrails, guardrails
SAML protocol
SAML is a standard that lets users sign in once and access multiple services by passing trusted identity messages between them.
also: SAML IDP
sandbox-escape technique
A technique where malware breaks out of a restricted, isolated testing environment to attack the real underlying system.
Sandboxing subsystem technique
A sandboxing subsystem is the part of software that isolates risky code so it cannot freely affect the rest of the system.
Sapporo Holdings organization
Sapporo Holdings is a Japanese beverage and brewing company.
Scattered Spider threat-actor
Scattered Spider is a cybercrime group known for social engineering, account theft, and high-profile intrusions.
also: Scattered Spider cybercrime group, Scattered Spider group, loosely affiliated, English-speaking Scattered Spider group, crew, group
scheduled task technique
A scheduled task is a job set to run automatically at a certain time or under certain conditions.
screenconnect
ScreenConnect, now ConnectWise Control, is a remote-support tool that attackers sometimes abuse for unauthorized access.
also: ScreenConnect remote access tool, ScreenConnect service
script.vbs tool
script.vbs is a Visual Basic Script file that can automate Windows actions and may be abused by attackers.
Search concept
Search is the process of finding information, such as files, web pages, or records, by looking for matching terms.
Search Engine Optimization concept
Search Engine Optimization is the practice of improving web content so search engines rank it higher.
also: SEO
secret_bytes.txt other
secret_bytes.txt is a text file name referenced in security reporting, likely used to store hidden or sensitive byte data.
Section 609(e) concept
Section 609(e) is a part of the Fair Credit Reporting Act that gives identity theft victims rights to obtain certain business records about fraudulent transactions.
Section 609(e) concept
Section 609(e) is a part of the Fair Credit Reporting Act that gives identity theft victims rights to obtain certain business records about fraudulent transactions.
Secure Future Initiative organization
Secure Future Initiative is Microsoft's company-wide program to improve the security of its products, engineering practices, and operations.
Secure Registry product
Secure Registry is a security-related product or service name referenced in reporting.
secure vibe coding concept
Secure vibe coding means using AI-assisted coding in a way that still checks for security problems instead of blindly trusting generated code.
SecureRepo tool
SecureRepo is a named repository, product, or tool referenced in security reporting.
Security concept
Security is the practice of protecting people, systems, data, and services from harm or unauthorized access.
security measures concept
Security measures are steps or tools used to reduce the chance or impact of security problems.
Security rules concept
Security rules are instructions that tell a system what activity to allow, block, alert on, or investigate.
Securonix organization
Securonix is a cybersecurity company known for threat detection, analytics, and security monitoring products.
Sekoia organization
Sekoia is a cybersecurity company known for threat intelligence and security operations products.
SendGrid product
SendGrid is an email delivery service used to send messages on behalf of other domains or organizations.
sensitive data concept
Sensitive data is information that could cause harm if exposed, such as passwords, financial details, or medical records.
also: critical data
SEO poisoning technique
SEO poisoning is an attack technique that manipulates search results to lead people to malicious websites.
SEO-poisoned technique
SEO-poisoned describes web content manipulated to rank highly in search results and lure people to malicious pages.
also: search engine optimization techniques
shadow-ai
Shadow AI is the use of AI tools in an organization without official approval, visibility, or security oversight.
Shadowserver organization
Shadowserver is an internet security watchdog group that tracks exposed systems and other security data.
sharepoint
SharePoint is Microsoft’s platform for storing, sharing, and collaborating on documents and internal websites.
shinyhunters
ShinyHunters is a cybercrime group known for stealing and selling large databases of user information.
Shor's algorithm concept
Shor's algorithm is a quantum computing algorithm that could break some widely used public-key cryptography if large enough quantum computers exist.
showDirectoryPicker() tool
showDirectoryPicker() is a browser feature that lets a website ask the user to choose a local folder.
showOpenFilePicker() tool
showOpenFilePicker() is a browser feature that lets a website ask the user to choose a local file.
SIEM product
A security tool that gathers and analyzes logs from across an organization in real time to spot suspicious activity.
sign-in keys concept
Sign-in keys are secret values or cryptographic keys used to log in or prove identity.
simonecorsi/mawesome tool
simonecorsi/mawesome is a GitHub repository or GitHub Action referenced in security reporting.
singapore
Singapore is a Southeast Asian country and technology hub often referenced in cybersecurity reporting.
SKILL.md other
SKILL.md is likely a markdown file used to describe an AI agent skill or capability.
Skills concept
Skills are plug-in-like capabilities or actions that an AI assistant or software agent can use to perform tasks.
also: agent skills
Skillspector tool
Skillspector is the name of a tool or project, likely used to inspect AI agent skills for security risks.
skytext tool
skytext is a named software package or tool referenced in security reporting.
Sleeman Breweries organization
Sleeman Breweries is a Canadian beer company referenced as an organization in security reporting.
slogsec tool
slogsec is a named software package or tool referenced in security reporting.
Slopsquatting technique
Slopsquatting is an attack where criminals register package names, domains, or links that AI tools have hallucinated so people may mistakenly use them.
SMS phishing technique
SMS phishing is phishing carried out through text messages to trick people into clicking links or giving up information.
SOC organization
SOC stands for security operations center, a team or function that monitors and responds to security issues.
also: SecOps
social engineering technique
Social engineering is manipulation of people into doing something or sharing information they otherwise would not.
also: fools people
Social Security numbers concept
Social Security numbers are U.S. government identification numbers often targeted by criminals because they can be used for fraud.
Socket Threat Research Team organization
Socket Threat Research Team is a security research team focused on software supply chain threats and malicious packages.
also: Socket
socket.io-client tool
socket.io-client is a JavaScript library used by web apps to communicate with Socket.IO servers in real time.
SOCRadar organization
SOCRadar is a cybersecurity company that provides threat intelligence and external attack surface monitoring services.
also: SOCRadar's Threat Research Unit, STRU, The researchers, The company
Software flaws vulnerability
Software flaws are mistakes or weaknesses in programs that can cause bugs or create security risks.
also: flaws, security problems
Sonnet 5 product
Sonnet 5 is a named version in the Sonnet family of Claude-style AI models.
Source IP correlation technique
Source IP correlation is the practice of comparing the internet addresses behind events to see whether they may be related.
spain other
Spain is a country in southwestern Europe.
Spam concept
Spam is unwanted bulk messages, usually sent by email, text, or online platforms.
Spam filters tool
Spam filters are tools that detect and block unwanted or suspicious messages.
also: spam filter
spear-phishing technique
Spear-phishing is a targeted scam message crafted for a specific person or organization to trick them into revealing information or taking an unsafe action.
spearphishing technique
Spearphishing is a targeted scam message sent to a specific person or group to trick them into giving access or information.
spearphishing-link
A spearphishing link is a targeted phishing message that includes a malicious link tailored to a specific person or organization.
Specially crafted requests technique
Specially crafted requests are deliberately designed inputs sent to software to trigger a bug or bypass protection.
SPF protocol
SPF is an email check that verifies whether a sending server is allowed to send email for a domain.
Static URL signatures technique
Static URL signatures are fixed patterns in web addresses that security tools use to recognize known malicious links.
steganography technique
Steganography is the practice of hiding secret data inside ordinary-looking files such as images or audio.
step-security/changed-files tool
step-security/changed-files is a GitHub Action-style tool or package referenced in software supply-chain security reporting.
stepsecurity organization
StepSecurity is a cybersecurity company focused on protecting software supply chains and CI/CD pipelines.
StepSecurity Maintained Actions tool
StepSecurity Maintained Actions are GitHub Actions maintained by StepSecurity with security-focused support or controls.
STOCKSTAY malware
STOCKSTAY is a named malware family or malware framework referenced in security reporting.
Stolen credit cards concept
Stolen credit cards are card numbers or physical cards taken or used without the owner's permission.
Stripe organization
Stripe is a company that provides online payment processing services for businesses.
Stripe FT3 tool
Stripe FT3 is a named fraud or payment-related tool, system, or dataset referenced in security reporting.
supply-chain-attacks technique
Supply-chain-attacks are attacks that target the software or service creation process so many downstream users can be affected.
also: supply-chain
Synacktiv organization
Synacktiv is a cybersecurity company known for security research, penetration testing, and vulnerability analysis.
T1204.004 technique
T1204.004 is a MITRE ATT&CK technique for tricking a user into copying and pasting malicious commands or content.
T1534 technique
A MITRE ATT&CK technique identifier for internal spearphishing, where an attacker with access to one account uses it to phish other members of the organization.
T1566.001 technique
A MITRE ATT&CK technique identifier for spearphishing attachments, a common initial access method where malicious files are sent via email.
Tag tampering technique
Tag tampering is altering labels, metadata, or tracking tags to mislead systems or hide what content really is.
taiwan other
Taiwan is a territory in East Asia that is often referenced in cybersecurity reporting involving regional targeting or geopolitical activity.
Talos organization
Talos, also known as Cisco Talos, is Cisco's threat intelligence and security research organization.
Taxpayer IDs concept
Taxpayer IDs are government-issued numbers used to identify people or businesses for tax purposes.
TCP Profile concept
A TCP Profile is a configuration object that controls how a device or service handles TCP network connections.
TCP TimeStamp protocol
TCP TimeStamp is a TCP feature that helps measure timing and improve reliability for network connections.
telegram
Telegram is a messaging app that supports channels, groups, bots, and encrypted chats.
Telegram bot tool
A Telegram bot is an automated account on the Telegram messaging platform that can respond to users or perform tasks.
Temperature setting concept
A temperature setting is a chosen value that controls how warm or cool a device or environment should be.
also: creativity setting
tenor product
Tenor is Google's GIF search and sharing service used by many apps and keyboards.
Tetrade malware
Tetrade is a label used in security reporting for a group of Latin American banking-trojan malware families.
The Wall Street Journal organization
The Wall Street Journal is a major U.S. newspaper that reports on business, finance, politics, and technology.
Themis product
Themis is described in the article as an IRONSCALES platform capability that evaluated the message.
third-party services concept
Third-party services are outside companies or platforms an organization uses to help run its business or technology.
also: third-party connections
Threat feeds tool
Threat feeds are streams of security information, such as malicious IP addresses or domains, used to help detect attacks.
threat intelligence concept
Threat intelligence is information collected and analyzed about possible or active cyber threats.
threat landscape concept
The overall picture of potential and existing cyber threats facing an individual, organization, or industry at a given time.
Tines product
Tines is a security automation platform used to build workflows for investigations, alerts, and response tasks.
tj-actions/changed-files tool
tj-actions/changed-files is a popular GitHub Action that identifies which files changed in a workflow.
TLS 1.3 protocol
TLS 1.3 is a modern version of the security protocol that encrypts many internet connections, including HTTPS websites.
tokens concept
Digital objects used for authentication, identification, or authorization, like access tokens.
Tom Brown other
Tom Brown is a named person referenced in security or technology reporting.
also: co-founder Tom Brown
Tomcat 10.1.37 product
Tomcat 10.1.37 is a specific version of the Apache Tomcat web server software.
Tomcat 11.0.5 product
Tomcat 11.0.5 is a specific version of the Apache Tomcat web server software.
Tomcat 9.0.101 product
Tomcat 9.0.101 is a specific version of the Apache Tomcat web server software.
tor
Tor is a privacy network that routes internet traffic through multiple relays to hide a user’s location.
traditional antivirus tool
Traditional antivirus is security software that looks for known malware, usually by matching files or behavior to known threats.
also: antivirus
traffic sniffers tool
Traffic sniffers are tools that capture and inspect data moving across a network.
Training concept
Training is the process of teaching people or systems new knowledge or skills.
Transaction records concept
Transaction records are stored details about purchases, payments, transfers, or other business activities.
also: records
transcript.pdf.js malware
transcript.pdf.js is a suspicious filename that looks like a PDF-related file but is actually a JavaScript file.
Transport for London organization
Transport for London is the public organization that runs much of London's transport system.
TripleDES protocol
TripleDES is an older encryption method that applies the DES cipher three times and is now generally considered outdated.
Trivy tool
Trivy is an open-source scanner used to find vulnerabilities and misconfigurations in containers, code, and cloud resources.
trivy-action tool
trivy-action is a GitHub Action that runs the Trivy security scanner inside GitHub workflows.
trojanized PoC exploits technique
Trojanized PoC exploits are proof-of-concept exploit files that have been secretly modified to include malware.
also: weaponized proof-of-concept (PoC) exploits, trojanised exploits
TRON protocol
TRON is a blockchain network used for cryptocurrency transactions and decentralized applications.
Trusted infrastructure concept
Trusted infrastructure is legitimate-looking or reputable services that attackers may abuse to make malicious activity seem normal.
trusted locations concept
Trusted locations are places, networks, or folders that an organization marks as safe for special access or fewer security checks.
also: non-trusted locations
Turla threat-actor
Turla is a long-running Russian-linked cyberespionage group known for stealthy attacks on governments and other high-value targets.
Twilio organization
Twilio is a cloud communications company that provides APIs for messaging, voice calls, and authentication.
typosquatting technique
Typosquatting is when attackers use names that look like legitimate websites or packages to trick people who make small typing mistakes.
U.S. casinos organization
U.S. casinos are gambling businesses located in the United States.
U.S. Commerce Department organization
The U.S. Commerce Department is a U.S. government department focused on trade, business, economic growth, and related policy.
U.S. Commerce Department organization
The U.S. Commerce Department is a U.S. government department focused on trade, business, economic growth, and related policy.
also: Commerce Department, his department
U.S. Department of Justice organization
The U.S. Department of Justice is the U.S. government department responsible for federal law enforcement and legal prosecutions.
U.S. Federal Trade Commission organization
The U.S. Federal Trade Commission is a government agency that enforces consumer protection, competition, privacy, and fraud rules.
also: FTC
UAC concept
UAC, or User Account Control, is a Windows feature that asks for permission before allowing high-privilege changes.
UK Financial Conduct Authority organization
The UK Financial Conduct Authority is the regulator that oversees financial firms and markets in the United Kingdom.
also: FCA
ukraine other
Ukraine is a country in Eastern Europe that is often discussed in cybersecurity because of major cyberattacks linked to regional conflict.
unauthenticated attacker concept
An attacker who does not have valid login credentials and attempts to access systems without authorization.
unauthenticated RCE vulnerability
Remote Code Execution that can be triggered without providing credentials, making it extremely dangerous.
unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability
Unauthenticated remote code execution is a serious flaw that lets an attacker run commands on a system over a network without logging in first.
Unauthorised access concept
Unauthorised access is when someone gets into an account, system, or data without permission.
Unauthorised purchases concept
Unauthorised purchases are transactions made without the account holder's permission.
UNC3944 threat-actor
UNC3944 is a threat-actor tracking name used for a group associated with social engineering, credential theft, and extortion activity.
Unit 42 organization
The threat intelligence and research team at Palo Alto Networks that publishes reports on cyber threats and vulnerabilities.
united-arab-emirates
