The United Arab Emirates is a Middle Eastern country that may be mentioned in cybersecurity stories as a location, target, or government context.
united-kingdom
The United Kingdom is a country often referenced in cybersecurity reporting as a target, regulator, or law-enforcement partner.
united-states
The United States is a country often referenced in cybersecurity stories as a target, source, regulator, or law-enforcement actor.
also: U.S.
University of Sydney organization
The University of Sydney is a major public university in Australia.
Unpatched flaw vulnerability
An unpatched flaw is a security weakness that has not yet been fixed on the affected system.
also: bug
Unpatched systems concept
Unpatched systems are computers or software that have not been updated with available security fixes.
unrelated-sender[.]example other
unrelated-sender[.]example is a defanged domain indicator used as an example or security artifact.
unrestricted-file-upload vulnerability
Unrestricted file upload is a vulnerability where a system fails to properly limit or check files that users can upload.
Unsanitized input vulnerability
Unsanitized input is user-provided data that has not been safely checked or cleaned before being used by software.
also: user-supplied input
URL-based filtering mechanisms technique
URL-based filtering mechanisms block or allow web traffic based on the website address being visited.
us-department-of-justice organization
The U.S. Department of Justice is the federal government department responsible for law enforcement and legal actions, including cybercrime cases.
USENIX organization
USENIX is a computing research association that publishes technical papers and runs conferences on systems, security, and related topics.
USENIX study organization
A USENIX study is academic or technical research published through the USENIX computing systems research community.
user accounts concept
User accounts are individual profiles that let people sign in and use computers, apps, or services.
also: Company F user accounts
User interaction concept
User interaction means a person has to do something, such as click a link or open a file, for an action or attack to happen.
user-account-control concept
User Account Control is a Windows feature that asks for permission before allowing actions that need higher system privileges.
username and password concept
A username and password are the basic login details a person uses to prove who they are to a system.
also: username/password combinations
VA OIG organization
VA OIG is the Office of Inspector General for the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs, which audits and investigates VA programs.
VBScript tool
VBScript is an older Microsoft scripting language that attackers sometimes abuse to run commands on Windows computers.
VEIL#DROP technique
VEIL#DROP is a named malware, tool, or threat activity label referenced in security reporting.
also: VEIL#DROP framework
verizon-dbir product
Verizon DBIR is Verizon's annual Data Breach Investigations Report that summarizes cybercrime and breach trends.
victim chats concept
Victim chats are private conversations between attackers and people or organizations they are targeting.
vietnam other
Vietnam is a country in Southeast Asia.
VirusTotal tool
VirusTotal is an online service that checks files, URLs, and other indicators against many security scanners and threat databases.
also: Google-owned malware scanning service, VT
Visibility plane concept
A visibility plane is the layer of tools or data that lets defenders see what is happening across systems.
Visual Basic Script tool
Visual Basic Script is an older Microsoft scripting language that can automate tasks and has also been abused to run malware.
Visual Studio Code tasks tool
Visual Studio Code tasks are configuration files that let VS Code run commands such as building, testing, or scripting a project.
VPN concept
A VPN is a tool that creates a private connection to a network over the internet.
VPN credentials concept
VPN credentials are the usernames, passwords, or keys used to connect to a private network remotely.
VPN gateways tool
VPN gateways are network devices or services that let users securely connect to a private network over the internet.
VS Code task files tool
VS Code task files are Visual Studio Code configuration files that define commands the editor can run automatically or on demand.
also: VS Code tasks, .vscode/tasks.json
vulnerabilities vulnerability
Vulnerabilities are weaknesses in software, hardware, or processes that attackers can exploit.
also: security flaws, issues, OpenClaw vulnerabilities
vulnerable concept
Vulnerable means a system or person has a weakness that could be exploited.
WatchTowr organization
WatchTowr is a security organization that publishes technical analysis and research.
watchTowr Labs organization
watchTowr Labs is a cybersecurity research organization known for vulnerability research and security analysis.
also: cybersecurity company
Web Application Components concept
Web application components are the parts, libraries, or services that make up a web application.
Webcam product
A webcam is a camera connected to or built into a computer or phone.
also: webcam and microphone feeds
Websites concept
Websites are pages or services on the internet that people access through a browser.
WebView product
WebView is a component that lets an app display web pages inside the app instead of opening a separate browser.
Whitespace padding technique
Whitespace padding is adding extra spaces, tabs, or blank lines to change how content looks to scanners without changing its meaning much.
windows product
Windows is Microsoft’s widely used operating system for personal computers and servers.
Windows 11 23H2 product
Windows 11 23H2 is a version release of Microsoft's Windows 11 operating system.
Windows 11 24H2 product
Windows 11 24H2 is a version update of Microsoft's Windows 11 operating system released for the second half of 2024.
Windows 11 25H2 product
Windows 11 25H2 is a version update of Microsoft's Windows 11 operating system expected for the second half of 2025.
Windows 11 26H1 product
Windows 11 26H1 is a named release version of Microsoft's Windows 11 operating system.
Windows Emoji Panel product
Windows Emoji Panel is a Windows feature that lets users insert emoji and symbols into text.
Windows Server product
Windows Server is Microsoft's operating system for servers used to run business applications, directories, and network services.
Windows systems product
Windows systems are computers running Microsoft's Windows operating system.
Windows Terminal product
Windows Terminal is Microsoft's command-line application for running shells such as PowerShell, Command Prompt, and WSL.
also: Terminal, WindowsTerminal.exe
windows-11
Windows 11 is Microsoft’s modern desktop operating system.
windows-script-host
Windows Script Host is a Microsoft feature that runs script files such as JavaScript and VBScript on Windows.
windows-server-2025 product
Windows Server 2025 is a Microsoft server operating system for running enterprise services and applications.
WinLocker malware
WinLocker is a type of malware that locks a Windows screen or system to pressure the victim, often for ransom.
also: ransomware 'WinLocker' screen
wscript-exe
wscript.exe is a Windows program that runs script files, and attackers often abuse it to execute malicious scripts.
X product
X is a social media platform formerly known as Twitter.
X-Antibot-Token protocol
X-Antibot-Token is an HTTP header or token name used to identify or enforce anti-bot checks.
XOR decryption technique
XOR decryption is using an XOR key to reveal data that was hidden with XOR obfuscation.
also: XOR-obfuscated payloads
XOR keys concept
XOR keys are values used to encode or decode data hidden with the XOR operation.
XOR obfuscation technique
XOR obfuscation is a simple way to hide data by mixing it with a key using the XOR operation.
XOR payload encryption technique
XOR payload encryption is a simple way attackers may disguise malicious code by scrambling it with an XOR operation until it is run.
also: XOR-encrypted payloads
xor-encryption technique
xor-encryption is a simple data-scrambling method based on the XOR operation, often used to hide content lightly.
xor-team threat-actor
xor-team is a named group or account referenced in security reporting.
Xpos587 other
Xpos587 is a named malware, tool, or activity label referenced in security reporting.
also: Xpos587 GitHub account
YouTube product
YouTube is Google’s video-sharing and streaming platform.
zero-click technique
Zero-click describes an attack that can work without the victim tapping, opening, or clicking anything.
Zero-click ecosystem concept
The zero-click ecosystem refers to the search and AI-answer environment where users get answers without clicking through to websites.
Zero-day bugs vulnerability
Zero-day bugs are software flaws that attackers may exploit before the developer has released a fix.
also: zero-day
Zero-day vulnerabilities vulnerability
Zero-day vulnerabilities are security flaws that are known to attackers or defenders before a patch is available.
also: zero-day flaw
zero-day vulnerability vulnerability
A zero-day vulnerability is a software flaw attackers can exploit before the vendor has released a fix.
also: previously undisclosed Nextcloud zero-day vulnerability
Zero-Impact Breach Prevention concept
Zero-Impact Breach Prevention is a security marketing term for preventing breaches before they cause business disruption.

No matching terms.

Suggest an improvement

DOWNLINK · 01 Jul 2026 00:00 UTC → 02 Jul 2026 00:00 UTC · 1 day window reports · sources · tour 50 articles · c2741f2
